title: Friendly failure when -d target can't be created + paste-safe README examples
action: User pasted the README greenfield example verbatim (-d /path/to/new-project); mkdir -p tried to create /path at root -> raw 'Permission denied'. Fix: (1) install.sh wraps the greenfield mkdir in a fail with guidance (real writable path, not the /path/to placeholder); (2) README bash example -> ~/projects/my-new-app, PowerShell -> C:\projects\my-new-app (paste-safe).

truths: bad -d exits 1 with the guidance message
prohibitions: do not change any other installer behavior

trace.outcome: Installer now fails with guidance when the -d target can't be created (was: raw mkdir 'Permission denied' after users paste the README placeholder verbatim); README examples switched to paste-safe paths.
trace.verification_evidence:
{ "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (user screenshot, repro'd locally): install.sh -d /path/to/new-project -y died with raw \"mkdir: cannot create directory '/path': Permission denied\" — the README greenfield example carried the literal placeholder, and -y auto-confirmed straight into mkdir.",
  "verified_truths": [
    "AFTER: bad -d exits 1 with: cannot create target directory ... Pass a real, writable path to -d — e.g. -d ~/projects/my-app — not a literal '/path/to/...' placeholder",
    "real -d to a tmp dir still installs greenfield end-to-end (bee.mjs landed)",
    "bash -n clean; README bash + PowerShell examples now paste-safe" ],
  "command": "bash -n scripts/install.sh + local-source smoke of both branches" }
trace.verify_output: syntax OK; smoke: -d /path/to/new-project => exit 1 + guidance message; -d <tmp> => greenfield install landed (local REPO_URL override)
